name: Potato Risotto with Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Pound Shiitake mushrooms or other fresh mushrooms, ends trimmed and thinly diced (1/4 to 1/2)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the potatoes by cutting them into thin, even slices using a knife or food processor with a slicing blade. Then cut the slices into 1/8 inch wide strips. Cut the strips crosswise into very fine dice the size of rice grains. Place the diced potatoes in a large bowl covered with cold water.
2. To cook, bring 1 cup of heavy cream to a boil in a heavy large nonstick saucepan over medium-high heat. Drain the potatoes and add them to the cream. Simmer gently, adjusting the heat as needed, until the cream is absorbed, stirring occasionally.
3. Pour in half of the remaining cream with all of the mushrooms. Simmer gently until the cream is absorbed, stirring occasionally. This step can be prepared up to this point about 1 1/2 hours ahead and set aside at room temperature.
4. To serve, bring to a simmer and pour in the remaining cream. Simmer until thickened and the cream is absorbed (the potatoes will start to meld together), for about 20 minutes. Stir in the Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Mound the risotto in a vegetable dish or spoon out onto 4 to 6 large plates with an entrée. Serve immediately.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings
